text: parent child navigation in aim search results
description: in aim search, let's introduce navigation feature:  h shows list of supported aims, l shos list of supporting aims. This is relevant, because sometimes we find an aim but then want to select one of it's children.

reflections.context: Enable better navigation between related aims directly within the search results modal.
reflections.outcome: Users can now traverse up (parents) and down (children) from any search result, and common path suffixes are automatically grouped.
reflections.effectiveness: Highly effective. h/l navigation feels natural for graph traversal, and path merging significantly reduces UI clutter in deep hierarchies.
reflections.lesson: Suffix-based path merging is a robust way to handle 'multiple paths to the same node' visualization issues.
